basics.name: Joe Pegler
basics.label: Founding Engineer | Web3 Infrastructure | Account Abstraction (ERC-4337)
basics.summary: Infrastructure engineer who owns critical systems end-to-end: transaction pipelines, bundlers, SDKs, and cross-chain execution. Built and shipped ERC-4337 tooling and smart-account platforms at scale; comfortable with ambiguity, 0→1 builds, and decisions without full information. TypeScript, Node.js, EVM/Solana. Cork, Ireland (GMT). Open to founding-engineer roles.

selectedImpact: Designed and shipped in-house ERC-4337 bundler with retries, EntryPoint v7 simulation, and structured error decoding—enabling gasless and sponsored flows and reducing transaction friction for users across EVM chains.
Owned flagship TypeScript SDK for ERC-4337 smart accounts and multi-chain execution at Biconomy; drove architecture, EIP-712/1271/7739/6492 signing (including P-256/passkeys), and developer adoption.

Built defi.app platform infrastructure: multi-chain quote/route orchestration (LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, LayerZero), action providers (Aave, Morpho, Hyperliquid), and EVM + Solana simulation and paymaster flows for a unified cross-chain product.
Led zero-to-one dApp and intents/solvers framework at Enso; owned full stack from frontend through NestJS backend and reliable execution paths for decentralized trades.
Shipped production-grade CI/CD and delivery practices for business-critical trading and analytics platforms; established reliability and team velocity (GameAnalytics MVP 2018).

work.name: defi.app
work.summary: Own infrastructure and developer tooling for the defi.app ecosystem. Ship reliable cross-chain systems and SDKs under ambiguity: in-house ERC-4337 bundling, multi-chain quote/execution orchestration, and DeFi action providers.
highlights: Designed and shipped in-house ERC-4337 bundler for latency-sensitive user-operation submission: retries, EntryPoint v7 simulation, and structured error decoding (Sourcify, custom ABIs), improving submission reliability and enabling gasless/sponsored flows.

Built platform infrastructure for multi-chain transactions: swap/bridge quote and route orchestration (LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, Relay), action providers (Aave, Morpho, Hyperliquid, Relay), and EVM + Solana simulation and paymaster flows for a single product surface.

Designed and own the defi.app TypeScript SDK: account abstraction (ZeroDev/Kernel, session keys), RouteClient and ExecutionManager for quote and execution, and provider integrations (Polymarket, Hyperliquid, Crossmint, Onramper) with backend-proxied APIs and integration tests.

Shipped DeFi action providers (Aave, Morpho, Aark, Hyperliquid, Relay, Transfer, Airdrop) for validation, gas/execution estimates, source-balance handling, and calldata population—reducing integration time and failure rates in swap and bridge flows.

Delivered Solana paymaster and EVM+SVM simulation for sponsored transactions and pre-execution validation (slippage, state overrides, Jito), enabling unified cross-chain UX without chain-specific handoffs.

work.name: biconomy
work.summary: Led the core TypeScript SDK for ERC-4337 smart accounts and multi-chain execution. Owned architecture, developer experience, and shipping under evolving EIP standards.
highlights: Led and shipped the flagship TypeScript SDK for ERC-4337 smart accounts and multi-chain execution; owned architecture and developer-facing API design used by teams building on account abstraction.
Built modular viem-based client with ERC-7579 decorators and Safe support; reduced integration complexity and improved type safety for smart-account and paymaster flows.
Implemented EIP-712/1271/7739/6492 signing, including P-256/passkeys, and delivered MEE quote/sign/execute, supertransactions, and session permissions—enabling gasless and keyless UX for end users.

Optimized gas and latency via bundler/paymaster integrations and concurrency; added composable batching, Cancun checks, and EntryPoint v0.7 support to keep the SDK production-ready as standards evolved.
Shipped robust HTTP client, typed errors, and documentation; drove CI with Vitest, Bun, GitHub Actions, and Codecov—improving reliability and time-to-ship for SDK consumers.

work.name: enso
work.summary: Led zero-to-one build of the main client-facing dApp on Ethereum. Owned full stack: frontend, NestJS backend, and intents/solvers framework for decentralized trades and actions.
highlights: Led development of the primary client-facing dApp on Ethereum; owned product from frontend through backend and execution layer, shipping under evolving requirements.
Built and maintained the NestJS backend; owned integration between frontend and backend and reliable execution paths with failure handling and retries.
Shipped and extended the intents and solvers framework for matching and execution of decentralized trades and actions—enabling intent-based UX and solver competition.

work.name: coinflex
work.summary: Owned the trading platform's core interface and delivery pipeline. Led a small team, set standards, and shipped fully automated CI/CD for the business-critical product.
highlights: Owned creation and evolution of the platform's trading interface; implemented core features, led code reviews, and raised stability and reusability.
Designed and ran fully automated CI/CD for the critical trading interface; established operational rigor and reduced deployment risk.

work.name: GameAnalytics
work.summary: Led frontend platform and team of five: set coding standards, tech stack, and delivery practices. Drove quality via TDD and mentorship; awarded company MVP 2018.
highlights: Owned coding standards and technology stack for a team of five; drove TDD and quality practices—improving platform reliability and delivery predictability.
Awarded company MVP 2018/19 for impact on product and team velocity.

work.name: FSI
work.summary: Delivered enterprise cross-platform mobile and web solutions; owned the drag-and-drop mobile designer and cross-platform JavaScript API. Led a sub-team as Scrum Master.
highlights: Owned the web-based drag-and-drop mobile designer and cross-platform JavaScript API; drove feature delivery and cross-functional execution.

work.name: Healthcare 21
work.summary: Owned end-to-end build of AssetTrak: mobile dispatch and productivity tracking plus real-time fleet monitoring portal. Shipped from design through full stack.
highlights: Designed and built AssetTrak from scratch: mobile tool for courier dispatch and productivity tracking, plus web portal for real-time fleet monitoring; owned full product delivery.

work.name: Nixatel
work.summary: Shipped two product builds in a small team: self-service health station app and pickacab (mobile payment and dispatch). Owned frontend and real-time stack (Node.js, Faye).
highlights: Shipped self-service health station app and pickacab (mobile payment and cloud-based dispatch); owned frontend and real-time stack with Node.js and Faye.
